Wisconsin

Getting Your Dietitian License in Wisconsin

Here's what you need to know.

Last updated 2026-02-25 · Sources verified

Quick reference

At a glance

Endorsement fee
$60
Processing time
2 to 4 Weeks
Renewal fee
$60
Fingerprinting
Required
Board
Dietitians Affiliated Credentialing Board
Path
Dietitian Compact enacted
See the steps Primary board page

Last updated 2026-02-25.

ENDORSEMENT PATH

Path for Out-of-State Dietitians

Wisconsin has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but the compact is not live for clinicians yet. For now, out-of-state applicants still use endorsement.

Wisconsin does not offer formal licensure by reciprocity. Out-of-state dietitians must apply through the standard process via the DSPS LicensE portal. Holding current CDR registration typically satisfies the education and examination requirements. Wisconsin's supervised practice hour requirements (900 hours with specific clinical/food service/community breakdowns) may differ from other states.

What can slow you down

State-Specific Friction

These are the pieces most likely to affect whether your contract timeline is realistic in Wisconsin.

  • Fingerprinting: Required. Out-of-state applicants use: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)
  • License verification: Wisconsin expects verification of your current out-of-state license before the file is complete.
  • Compact status: Dietitian Compact is enacted in Wisconsin, but it is not issuing privileges yet.
  • Temporary license: The board page does not list a temporary path for this route.
  • Out-of-state fingerprint delay: 2-4 additional weeks can show up if you are completing prints outside Wisconsin.

Submission checklist

What Wisconsin Wants From You

This is the endorsement checklist for already-licensed clinicians moving into Wisconsin.

  1. Step 1

    Start the Wisconsin application

    Apply through Dietitians Affiliated Credentialing Board and plan on $60 in application fees.

  2. Step 2

    Send verification of your current license

    Wisconsin expects verification sent directly from your current state or its verification system before the file is complete.

  3. Step 3

    Send Official College Transcripts

    From a regionally accredited institution, showing degree in an approved field.

  4. Step 4

    Send CDR Registration Verification or Exam Results

    Proof of passing the CDR registration examination or current CDR credential.

  5. Step 5

    Send Supervised Practice Documentation

    Verification of 900+ hours, including breakdown of clinical (300 hrs), food service (120 hrs), and community nutrition (120 hrs) components.

  6. Step 6

    Complete fingerprinting or background check

    Wisconsin lists fingerprinting or background screening on this path. Out-of-state applicants use: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects).

  7. Step 7

    Wait for board processing

    Most applicants should plan on 2 to 4 Weeks.

Costs and timing

Fees and Renewal

Total fee
$60
Processing time
2 to 4 Weeks
Renewal fee
$60
Renewal cycle
Every 2 years (by Oct 31, even years)
Application
$60
Background check
$0 (included in application)
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$60

License options

License Types

Certified Dietitian (CD): Wisconsin's credential for practicing dietitians, issued by the Dietitians Affiliated Credentialing Board under DSPS. The state is considering transitioning to 'Licensed Dietitian Nutritionist (LDN)' under pending legislation.

Paperwork

Required Documents

  • Official College Transcripts: From a regionally accredited institution, showing degree in an approved field.
  • CDR Registration Verification or Exam Results: Proof of passing the CDR registration examination or current CDR credential.
  • Supervised Practice Documentation: Verification of 900+ hours, including breakdown of clinical (300 hrs), food service (120 hrs), and community nutrition (120 hrs) components.

Board contact

Board Contact

Board
Dietitians Affiliated Credentialing Board
Department
Wisconsin Department of Safety and Professional Services (DSPS)
Phone
(608) 266-2112
Board site
Primary board page
Compact source
Dietitian Compact

Source: Primary board website

Renewal

Renewal

Renewal fee is $60. Renewal cycle: Every 2 years (by Oct 31, even years).

Questions

Wisconsin Dietitian Licensing FAQ

How much does a Wisconsin dietitian certification cost?

The initial credential fee is $60. Renewal is also $60, due by October 31 of even-numbered years. Late renewal after five years incurs an additional $25 fee.

What are Wisconsin's supervised practice hour requirements?

Wisconsin requires a minimum of 900 hours of supervised dietetics practice, including at least 300 hours in clinical dietetics, 120 hours in food service administration, and 120 hours in community nutrition — all under approved supervision.

Is Wisconsin transitioning to licensure?

Yes. There are active legislative discussions about transitioning from 'certification' to 'licensure,' which would change the credential to Licensed Dietitian Nutritionist (LDN) and potentially update fees. Monitor the DSPS website for updates.

Does Wisconsin have reciprocity for dietitians?

Wisconsin has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, dietitians still apply by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Wisconsin?

Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

Can I start working in Wisconsin while my endorsement application is processing?

Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.
